summary: Partially decompiled Sheik chain item code, replacing several `itseakchain` stubs/UNK prototypes with typed implementations and improving supporting chain-link structures and call sites. The bot reported 3 new matches in `main/melee/it/items/itseakchain`: `it_802BCFC4`, `fn_802BB44C`, and `it_802BCA30`, plus near-matches for `it_802BCB88`, `fn_802BB574`, and `it_802BBB0C`. The PR also standardized many `Point3d*` uses to `Vec3*` and clarified `ItemLink` field names used by hookshot/seakchain-style linked item segments.
what_changed: Implemented `fn_802BB44C`, `fn_802BB574`, `it_802BBB0C`, `it_802BCB88`, and `it_802BCFC4` in `itseakchain.c` where several had previously been `/// #...` placeholders.
Changed `it_802BCFC4` from a wide guessed signature taking `float*`, `itChainSegment*`, `Item*`, and an extra float to the simpler typed signature `void it_802BCFC4(Item_GObj* gobj, Vec3* vel)`.

Updated `ftSk_SpecialS_CheckInitChain` so Sheik side-special chain startup passes a `Vec3` velocity to `it_802BCFC4`, deriving `vec0.x` from `itChainSegment->x50` and multiplying by item facing direction.

Refined `ItemLink` in `itCharItems.h`: `x8_vel` became `vel`, `x20_vec` became `x20`, `x30_collData` became `coll_data`, `x1D0_GObj` became `gobj`, `x1D4_JObj` became `jobj`, and bitfields were renamed to offset-style names such as `x2C_b0`.
Expanded `itSeakChain_Attrs` from mostly padding to include `x0`, `x4`, `x54`, and `x58`, with a `STATIC_ASSERT` for size `0x5C`.
Updated hookshot and common item-link code to use the refined `ItemLink` field names throughout `itlinkhookshot.c` and `it_2725.c`.

Corrected many prototypes and locals from `Point3d` to `Vec3`, including camera helpers, Yoshi special code, CPU attack code, stage headers, boomerang/kyasarin/dinfire item APIs, and `jobj.h` related declarations.

Added stack-layout helper structs in `itseakchain.static.h` for newly worked functions such as `_m2c_stack_fn_802BB574`, `_m2c_stack_fn_802BB44C`, `_m2c_stack_it_802BCB88`, and `_m2c_stack_it_802BCFC4`.
smart_moves: Matched item behavior by first cleaning the shared `ItemLink` layout and names, then applying those improved field names across both Sheik chain and Link hookshot code.
Reduced an overfit `it_802BCFC4` prototype to a natural `Vec3* vel` interface, which made the Sheik caller and item implementation line up more cleanly.
Used `Vec3` consistently instead of `Point3d` where the data is just a three-float vector, avoiding misleading type distinctions and helping prototypes converge.
Preserved stack-sensitive matching details with `PAD_STACK`, nested scopes, explicit `Mtx`/`Vec3` locals, and static stack structs in the `.static.h` file.
Kept an explicit TODO noting a likely shared inline among `fn_802BB784`, `fn_802BB574`, and `fn_802BB44C` rather than pretending the repeated matrix/vector setup was fully understood.
Added `STATIC_ASSERT` coverage for refined structs so future field discovery does not silently break item data layout.
decomp_lessons: Before matching a chain/rope-like item function, make the shared link-node struct readable and offset-checked; clearer `vel`, `pos`, `coll_data`, `gobj`, and `jobj` names make both caller and callee reconstruction easier.
If a decompiled caller is passing many pieces of data into a callee, re-check the callee signature; the real ABI may only take an object plus a pointer to a small aggregate like `Vec3`.

For matrix-derived item segment positions, matching often depends on explicit `PSMTXIdentity`, setting translation elements, `HSD_JObjSetupMatrix`, `PSMTXConcat`, and then copying individual `mtx` entries into a `Vec3`.
Offset-style bitfield names such as `x2C_b0` are preferable to guessed semantic names like `flag0` when behavior is not yet known.
Use `GET_FIGHTER`/`GET_ITEM` consistently when it helps reproduce expected codegen and improves type clarity over raw `gobj->user_data` access.
When partially matching a family of near-identical functions, document suspected shared inlines and keep stack metadata nearby for later work.
naming_conventions: `ItemLink::gobj` and `ItemLink::jobj` for object pointers previously named `x1D0_GObj` and `x1D4_JObj`.
Unknown bitfields on `ItemLink` use offset/bit naming, e.g. `x2C_b0` through `x2C_b7`, instead of guessed `flagN` names.
Unknown fields in `itSeakChain_Attrs` remain offset-based names such as `x0`, `x4`, `x54`, and `x58` until semantics are known.
Prefer `Vec3*` over `Point3d*` for generic 3D vector/position parameters when the codebase treats them as the same vector data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Inserted `PAD_STACK(2 * 4)`, `PAD_STACK(1 * 4)`, and similar padding to reproduce stack layout in `ftSk_SpecialS_CheckInitChain` and `it_802BCFC4`.
Used nested lexical scopes to influence local variable lifetimes and stack placement.
Used `static Vec3 const vec0_init = { 1.8f, 0.0f, 0.0f }` in the Sheik caller, with explicit float suffixes.
Added `_m2c_stack_*` structs with `STATIC_ASSERT` sizes in `itseakchain.static.h` to capture stack-frame evidence for partially matched functions.
Built chain segment world positions through explicit matrix operations: identity matrix, translation element assignment, jobj matrix setup, matrix concat, then `Vec3` extraction.
Replaced broad `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` declarations with typed return values and parameters for newly matched or improved functions.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided slice.
The decomp-dev bot reported matched code improvement for GALE01: +968 matched bytes and +0.02% matched code.
Bot-reported new matches: `it_802BCFC4` (+328 bytes), `fn_802BB44C` (+296 bytes), and `it_802BCA30` (+5 bytes) in `main/melee/it/items/itseakchain`.
Bot-reported improvements in unmatched `itseakchain` items: `it_802BCB88` to 99.35%, `fn_802BB574` to 99.82%, and `it_802BBB0C` to 85.27%.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully match `it_802BCB88`, `fn_802BB574`, or `it_802BBB0C`.
Search for subsequent renames or semantic discoveries for `ItemLink::x2C_b0` through `x2C_b7`.
Check whether the suspected shared inline among `fn_802BB784`, `fn_802BB574`, and `fn_802BB44C` was later extracted.
Look for later refinements of `itSeakChain_Attrs` fields `x0`, `x4`, `x54`, and `x58`.
Search for other PRs replacing `Point3d` with `Vec3` to identify broader type cleanup patterns.
